title: SoFi Technologies: Strong Earnings Met with Market Skepticism
text: SoFi Technologies delivered impressive Q1 2026 results—beating revenue estimates by 4%, doubling net income year-over-year, and posting its tenth consecutive profitable quarter—yet the stock fell 2.13% on April 28. The company reported $1.087 billion in adjusted net revenue with a 31% EBITDA margin and a Rule of 40 score of 72. Net income reached $167 million, more than double the prior year's $71 million. Membership grew substantially, with 1.1 million new members in Q1, bringing total membership to 14.7 million, up 35%. Product penetration also accelerated, with 43% of new products opened by existing members—up from 36% previously. CFO Chris Lapointe provided crucial clarity by revealing that 100% of our reported adjusted net revenue was cash revenue in both 2024 and 2025. This directly addressed accusations from short-seller Muddy Waters Research claiming the company inflated earnings through accounting practices. Personal loan net charge-off rates remained stable at 4.4% annualized, excluding delinquent loan sales. The 90-day delinquency rate was 47 basis points, showing improvement. Management presented cohort data demonstrating that recent vintage loans are performing substantially better than historical comparables. At $16.43, SoFi trades at 25.42x forward earnings, significantly higher than comparable lenders like Ally Financial. The article suggests SoFi is priced like a lender the market no longer trusts, but the Q1 numbers show a platform business with accelerating cross-sell.
